rules.reason: Core auth session endpoints are public or token/cookie-gated by design and have dedicated auth/rate-limit test coverage outside the high-risk route registry.

The per-cluster agent POSTs batched audit.k8s.io events to this ingest endpoint, gated on the cluster:update RBAC permission and idempotent on (cluster_id, auditID); it carries no high-risk blast radius beyond the owning cluster.

Mint/revoke the static bearer tokens the /scim/v2 provisioning chain authenticates against; the routes sit inside the JWT auth chain and are superuser-gated inside the handler (RequireSuperuser), the same posture as /admin/settings. The plaintext token is returned once on create and only its SHA-256 hash is persisted, with create/revoke audited via RecordAuditFromRequest.

Trigger a durable agent-token rotation (sets rotation_pending_at; the grace rotation completes on the agent's next CONNECT and never invalidates the token the agent currently holds) or hard-revoke the token (revoked_at) for one cluster. Both are gated on the clusters:update RBAC permission plus the write-clusters scope, blast radius is the owning cluster only, and each call is audited (agent.token.rotate.requested / agent.token.revoked).
